title: Christening

jokes.text: I heard your mama has more bacteria per square inch
jokes.setup: Jim explains keyboard bacteria statistics in serious health presentation
jokes.punchline: Employee responds with 'your mama' joke interruption
jokes.explanation: Basic interruption humor that derails serious moment with juvenile joke

jokes.text: Oh, no, it's just that, if a vampire had to cough, He would do it like this. Right, And ruin their cloaks? Do you have any idea how expensive wool is In Transylvania? 'cause of the euro.
jokes.setup: Kevin asks about 'vampire cough' technique
jokes.punchline: Creed escalates to specific concerns about Transylvanian wool costs and European economics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - takes vampire premise seriously with absurd economic concern. Character-specific absurdist logic.

jokes.text: They would set up hand-desanitizing stations. A simple bowl at every juncture filled with dirt, vomit, Fecal matter.
jokes.setup: Dwight argues against hand sanitizer for immune system strength
jokes.punchline: Proposes deliberate contamination stations with increasingly disgusting substances
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight extreme logic taken to absurd conclusion. Perfect character integration with his health theories.

jokes.text: So by that rationale, if I had to sneeze, I should just sneeze on you. Yes. I would welcome it. You're welcome.
jokes.setup: Jim tests Dwight's germ exposure theory with direct challenge
jokes.punchline: Dwight welcomes it, Jim immediately sneezes on him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim/Dwight dynamic - Jim sets logical trap, Dwight falls into it, Jim immediately exploits it.

jokes.text: I don't know why everyone doesn't do this. Maybe they have something against living forever.
jokes.setup: Dwight's talking head about his germ exposure theory
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion that his method leads to immortality
jokes.explanation: Dwight's deadpan confidence in completely absurd health theory. Character-perfect arrogance.

jokes.text: Top o' the Sunday morning to you. And a top o' the day to you too, sir. I hope you brought your pipes. We're about to smoke the opium of the masses.
jokes.setup: Andy greets Creed with Irish accent at church
jokes.punchline: Creed responds thinking they're literally smoking opium (Marx reference)
jokes.explanation: Creed misinterprets Marx's 'opium of the masses' literally. Character-appropriate confusion.

jokes.text: I invited everyone in the office Because it's not a day for just Jim Or Pam or the baby or me. It is about us all.
jokes.setup: Michael explains why he invited entire office to christening
jokes.punchline: Makes baby's christening about himself and everyone else
jokes.explanation: Michael's narcissistic need to make every occasion about himself and 'family'

jokes.text: Does the nard dog want nard pups? Yeah. I want a big old litter of nard pups, All jumping around and sucking on the teat. Put 'em in a box, give 'em to my neighbors.
jokes.setup: Andy asks himself about wanting kids using his nickname
jokes.punchline: Extends dog metaphor to disturbing literal details about nursing and giving away children
jokes.explanation: Andy's inappropriate extension of his dog persona to family planning gets uncomfortable

jokes.text: But I'm the godfather. It is really funny, but you know That can be confusing at a christening, Because you are not the godfather. Are you talking to me?
jokes.setup: Michael claims to be godfather, Jim corrects him
jokes.punchline: Michael does De Niro impression completely missing the point
jokes.explanation: Michael deflects serious correction with movie reference, classic avoidance behavior

jokes.text: Old friend, like, you've known him since kindergarten? No, I met his wife in mommy and me.
jokes.setup: Michael probes about how long they've known the real godfather
jokes.punchline: Reveals shallow recent connection through parenting class
jokes.explanation: Michael's jealousy exposed through interrogation about friendship depth

jokes.text: Cece is turning out To be a little b-I-t-c-h. And that's not true, But her parents are Ki.
jokes.setup: Michael's talking head about being excluded from christening plans
jokes.punchline: Calls baby a bitch then backtracks, calls parents 'Ki' (incomplete insult)
jokes.explanation: Michael's petty reaction shows his immaturity but execution is weak and mean-spirited

jokes.text: If you go to Cabo San Lucas, All the prostitutes are from Cabo Corrientes.
jokes.setup: Pastor mentions youth ministry going to Cabo Corrientes
jokes.punchline: Creed loudly shares inappropriate prostitution knowledge in church
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - inappropriate personal knowledge shared at worst possible time and place

jokes.text: I would like to offer A 4% discount on all Dunder Mifflin Sabre products If you buy a printer at full price
jokes.setup: Dwight takes church prayer request opportunity
jokes.punchline: Turns it into sales pitch with pathetic discount offer
jokes.explanation: Dwight's inability to read social situations leads to sales pitch in sacred space

jokes.text: Somebody needs a change. Right now? Well, she can't bring this up with her.
jokes.setup: Baby needs changing during christening ceremony
jokes.punchline: Timing is worst possible moment during religious service
jokes.explanation: Standard baby comedy timing but nothing particularly clever about execution

jokes.text: Cece, no. No, no, no... not on the dress. Cece, stop.
jokes.setup: Baby being changed in church vestibule
jokes.punchline: Baby ruins christening dress with bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Generic baby bodily function humor, predictable and not particularly clever

jokes.text: We have an extra outfit in the bag. No. There's no extra outfit in the bag. You said you checked it. I did...Say that.
jokes.setup: Parents need backup outfit for soiled christening dress
jokes.punchline: Neither parent actually packed one despite claiming they did
jokes.explanation: Realistic parenting fail moment that creates awkward situation but not particularly funny

jokes.text: It's as if they leave my office and they go to another office That sells happiness. And good for them. That's great, because, you know, The paper industry's not gonna last forever.
jokes.setup: Michael's talking head about Jim and Pam's family success
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that their happiness won't last because paper industry is doomed
jokes.explanation: Michael's jealousy masked as concern with darkly funny industry pessimism

jokes.text: Okay, 'a, ' "halbert"? And, 'b, ' I think a more appropriate statement would be, "the halberts are hosting an intimate reception For their invited guests."
jokes.setup: Pastor mispronounces their name and mentions reception
jokes.punchline: Jim corrects pronunciation and emphasizes reception is for invited guests only
jokes.explanation: Jim's polite but firm attempt to control the guest list disaster

jokes.text: For all their generosity of spirit, They password-protect their wireless? Try Jesus. Opus Dei.
jokes.setup: Ryan comments on hosts' generosity
jokes.punchline: Immediately complains about wifi password, suggests religious passwords
jokes.explanation: Ryan's entitled millennial behavior at someone else's reception with clever religious password guesses

jokes.text: I am feeling this. Call it the Holy spirit or the passion of the Christ. I am loving these people.
jokes.setup: Michael in church environment
jokes.punchline: Conflates religious feeling with generic social networking energy
jokes.explanation: Michael's superficial engagement with religious concepts for social purposes

jokes.text: Oh, well, Dave is an adorable baby. Davey. Yeah. Wow.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to make small talk about someone's baby
jokes.punchline: Gets baby's name wrong, awkward silence follows
jokes.explanation: Michael's social awkwardness in trying to connect with strangers

jokes.text: We're gonna need A loaves and fishes kind of miracle To feed 'em all. Jesus is not your caterer.
jokes.setup: Doug jokes about needing biblical miracle to feed unexpected crowd
jokes.punchline: Angela responds with characteristically literal religious correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela response - literal interpretation of religious metaphor with disapproving tone

jokes.text: But he should be your caterer, 'cause you're a little angel. Why didn't your parents get you a caterer?
jokes.setup: Michael responds to Angela's Jesus comment
jokes.punchline: Calls her angel then immediately criticizes her parents' reception planning
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate personal comment followed by criticism lacks comedic structure

jokes.text: They don't think. Don't listen to her. She's just jealous 'cause she doesn't get to come home To someone as cute as you.
jokes.setup: Angela criticizes her own parents to baby
jokes.punchline: Michael assumes she's jealous of baby's relationship status
jokes.explanation: Michael misreads situation and makes inappropriate assumption about Angela's personal life

jokes.text: Oh, no, after you. No, I insist. After me. I'm gonna use that one.
jokes.setup: Standard polite door-holding exchange
jokes.punchline: Andy subverts with 'after me' then admits it's a line he's stealing
jokes.explanation: Andy's social awkwardness in trying out new 'smooth' lines and immediately revealing the attempt

jokes.text: This is not the meal I was promised. I'm going to have no energy for the rest of the day. I canceled my plans to come To this thing, and they repay me with this?
jokes.setup: Stanley surveys the modest reception food
jokes.punchline: Elaborate complaints about free meal at someone else's christening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley behavior - entitled complaints about free food with complete selfishness

jokes.text: Teach for America girls are way hotter, But they're nuts.
jokes.setup: Discussion of youth ministry volunteers going to Mexico
jokes.punchline: Ryan reduces missionary work to dating opportunities with crude generalization
jokes.explanation: Ryan's inappropriate objectification shows his shallow worldview but joke itself is crude

jokes.text: Who takes a kid to Mexico? I would run to Mexico if that's where the sandwiches are.
jokes.setup: Discussion of childhood service trip to Mexico
jokes.punchline: Kevin's simple food-motivated travel philosophy
jokes.explanation: Kevin reduces complex international service to simple food motivation - perfectly in character

jokes.text: What if the moon was your car And Jupiter was your hair brush?
jokes.setup: Youth minister makes earth/building family metaphor
jokes.punchline: Kevin extends metaphor to completely absurd celestial objects as personal items
jokes.explanation: Kevin's absurdist logic takes metaphor to brilliant nonsensical extreme. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I think that was sconesy cider, Noted baptism reception critic.
jokes.setup: Someone complains about reception food quality
jokes.punchline: Jim creates fake food critic persona combining the available items
jokes.explanation: Jim's quick wit creates memorable fake food critic name from reception items

jokes.text: Let's go help Africa. Let's go build an airport. We'll start small. We'll have a carwash. We'll send some cheerleaders to regionals.
jokes.setup: Michael wants office to do charity work together
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly random and unrelated 'charitable' activities
jokes.explanation: Michael's stream-of-consciousness charity ideas escalate from global to bizarrely specific

jokes.text: Yeah, well, that's just a pick-up scene.
jokes.setup: Oscar mentions volunteering at clinic
jokes.punchline: Michael reduces genuine charity work to dating opportunity
jokes.explanation: Michael's cynical view of volunteer work shows his shallow understanding of altruism

jokes.text: Everybody have their kool-aid? No. Okay, you know what? This is inappropriate.
jokes.setup: Michael makes cult reference about church gathering
jokes.punchline: Angela immediately shuts down the Jonestown reference
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate cult reference at religious gathering gets proper correction from Angela

jokes.text: You're mean. You're mean girls. Like the movie mean girls. And Kevin and Stanley, If you don't stop worrying so much about what you're gonna put In your gullet, you're gonna die in about a month.
jokes.setup: Michael lashes out at coworkers for not appreciating reception
jokes.punchline: Calls them Mean Girls then threatens Kevin and Stanley with food-related death
jokes.explanation: Michael's petty outburst shows his insecurity but lacks comedic sophistication

jokes.text: I guess I'll watch Suzanne's purse and your baby.
jokes.setup: Pam asks Meredith to watch baby while she gets chairs
jokes.punchline: Meredith equates baby care with purse-watching duty
jokes.explanation: Meredith's casual equating of baby with purse shows her detached parenting perspective

jokes.text: Hey. Hey. Is this the bus to Mexico? Yeah! I'll take a shirt. Are you coming? If you'll have me, yes. Heck, yes.
jokes.setup: Michael sees youth ministry bus leaving
jokes.punchline: Impulsively decides to join Mexican mission trip with no preparation
jokes.explanation: Michael's impulsive escape from social discomfort escalates to international commitment

jokes.text: You don't need one to get in. Plus, we can just have it sent to him down in Mexico.
jokes.setup: Jim points out Michael needs passport for Mexico
jokes.punchline: Youth minister suggests mailing passport to Mexico, missing the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Youth minister's naive logistics solution shows well-meaning but impractical idealism

jokes.text: Tell them that I died And I turned into an angel, And, when they feel a breeze In a room with all the windows closed Or that... that chill on the back of their neck, That is just me watching over them.
jokes.setup: Jim asks what to tell clients about Michael's absence
jokes.punchline: Michael creates elaborate angel mythology for his Mexico trip explanation
jokes.explanation: Michael's romantic self-image extends to creating ghost story for business absence

jokes.text: Tell them that I'm in a meeting.
jokes.setup: After elaborate angel explanation, Jim asks about corporate accounts
jokes.punchline: Michael gives completely mundane business excuse for same situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation from romantic angel story to boring corporate excuse

jokes.text: I think it's really cool, Michael. I wish I had a job that I could just leave.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing his Mexico departure
jokes.punchline: Ryan envies ability to abandon responsibilities, missing irony of his own work ethic
jokes.explanation: Ryan's comment reveals his desire to avoid responsibility while already being irresponsible

jokes.text: Save me an aisle seat, Michael! I'm coming! I will not stand idly by While these Mexican villagers are sick. We're actually building 'em a school. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Dwight decides to join Michael on Mexico trip
jokes.punchline: Creates false medical emergency, dismisses correction about actual mission
jokes.explanation: Dwight's heroic self-image overrides actual facts about mission purpose

jokes.text: Nobody I know would leave their jobs And friends and families To do manual labor for three months. Well, you know what, everybody thinks that I am crazy, And that tells me that I am the sanest person I know.
jokes.setup: Michael reflects on his impulsive Mexico decision
jokes.punchline: Uses others' concern as proof of his superior judgment
jokes.explanation: Michael's twisted logic that opposition proves wisdom shows his defensive self-justification

jokes.text: Uh, meemaw, where's Cece? I don't know. I lost the purse too.
jokes.setup: Pam returns to find Meredith was watching baby
jokes.punchline: Meredith casually admits losing both baby and purse with equal concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to purse/baby comparison with escalated stakes and Meredith's casual delivery

jokes.text: How long till we get to Mexico? Well, two days minus how long we've been on the road. 45 minutes? So, like, two days basically.
jokes.setup: Michael asks about travel time to Mexico
jokes.punchline: Comically poor math skills in calculating remaining journey time
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to do basic subtraction shows his impulsive decision-making

jokes.text: Why aren't they building it themselves? They don't know how. Do we know how? I don't know how.
jokes.setup: Discussion of building school for Mexican village
jokes.punchline: Realization that American volunteers also lack construction skills
jokes.explanation: Logical question exposes the absurdity of unqualified volunteers doing skilled labor

jokes.text: Oh, I feel like there were so many more people on this bus. There were, like, 200, 300 people before.
jokes.setup: Michael notices the bus seems less crowded
jokes.punchline: Wildly inflated estimate of previous passenger count on standard bus
jokes.explanation: Michael's poor observational skills and tendency to exaggerate simple situations

jokes.text: Do you smell like a weird, warm cheese?
jokes.setup: Michael getting uncomfortable on bus journey
jokes.punchline: Notices unpleasant odor with specific cheese comparison
jokes.explanation: Standard travel discomfort humor with mildly specific descriptive detail

jokes.text: Oh, God, you know what? I am gonna be in Mexico for my half-birthday. And the Christmas party. And cinco De Mayo.
jokes.setup: Michael realizes timeline implications of three-month trip
jokes.punchline: Mentions missing made-up holiday and ironically missing Cinco de Mayo while in Mexico
jokes.explanation: Michael's priorities revealed through what events he'll miss, including ironic Cinco de Mayo

jokes.text: Construction projects, they say three months, And then, after two years, The lazy bums haven't even started. Now we're those lazy bums.
jokes.setup: Michael worries about construction timeline
jokes.punchline: Realizes he and Dwight will become the lazy contractors they complain about
jokes.explanation: Michael's self-awareness about becoming the problem he usually criticizes

jokes.text: Jim, Jim, I want to give a toast. Where's the little girl of honor? I don't know, dad. I don't know.
jokes.setup: Jim's father wants to toast at christening reception
jokes.punchline: Jim has to admit he doesn't know where his own baby is
jokes.explanation: Jim's parenting anxiety and embarrassment in front of his father creates cringe moment

jokes.text: Stop that tiny, blonde woman! She stole my baby! What are you doing? Give me that baby. What? Kevin!
jokes.setup: Pam panics about missing baby and sees small blonde woman
jokes.punchline: Discovers it's Kevin in wig changing the baby
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Kevin as the 'tiny blonde woman' - visual comedy that works

jokes.text: Did you think I stole your baby? What's that? Oh, yeah, back then I did. Just now. So don't, because I've got my eye on you.
jokes.setup: Angela asks if Pam suspected her of baby theft
jokes.punchline: Pam admits she did and maintains suspicious vigilance
jokes.explanation: Pam's honest admission of suspicion creates awkward social moment

jokes.text: What kind of a person steals scones From a baby? Someone put them in my bag.
jokes.setup: Jim discovers scones in Angela's bag at reception
jokes.punchline: Angela claims someone else put them there, implausible denial
jokes.explanation: Angela's weak excuse for hoarding reception food shows her selfishness

jokes.text: Can you fake a seizure or a heart attack or something? I can make myself cry. Do that. Do it.
jokes.setup: Michael wants to get off Mexico bus
jokes.punchline: Asks Dwight to fake medical emergency, settles for crying
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperation leads to asking Dwight for fake medical emergency assistance

jokes.text: Okay, driver, driver, if you're not gonna stop this bus, Then I'm going to stop it for you. Okay? Hang on. Stopping in Tennessee.
jokes.setup: Michael threatens to stop the bus himself
jokes.punchline: Immediately backs down when driver agrees to stop
jokes.explanation: Michael's empty threat immediately resolved when he gets what he wants

jokes.text: Is anyone still at the church? No. Actually, most of them went to go see a movie. What? Shut up. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Michael returns from Mexico adventure expecting reception to continue
jokes.punchline: Discovers everyone abandoned reception for movie, Michael's disbelief
jokes.explanation: Ironic ending where Michael's grand gesture is upstaged by everyone choosing entertainment over his return

tmdb_overview: Pam and Jim prepare for Cece's baptism; Jim worries Angela has taken his daughter.
